etymology.description: 門(門) (mén, "door") + 卝(卝) (guàn, "locked door; two wooden sticks to lock a door") -> Phono-semantic: semantic 門(門) (mén, "door") + phonetic 𢇅
etymology.interpretation: The character represents a door locked with two wooden sticks, reflecting the concept of being closed or secured. The phonetic component, originally depicted with rings resembling 幺 (yāo), contributes to the pronunciation.
etymology.simplification: 关(關): The character was simplified by removing the more complex 卝(卝) component, leaving only the 门(門) (mén, "door") radical.

parts.english: locked door; two wooden sticks to lock a door

examples.chinese: 请把门关上(請把門關上)。
我关心你的健康(我關心你的健康)。
这件事情与你无关(這件事情與你無關)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè jiàn shìqíng yǔ nǐ wúguān.
examples.english: This matter has nothing to do with you.
